seoTitle: How to Add an Acceptance Criteria Field in Jira
metaDescription: Add a dedicated acceptance criteria field to Jira issues so stories have a clear, consistent definition of done.
intro: Without a defined place to record acceptance criteria, teams often bury it in the description or skip it entirely, leading to unclear expectations. This guide covers setting up a proper acceptance criteria field in Jira.

steps.heading: Open custom fields settings
steps.body: Go to Jira administration and find the custom fields section where new fields are created.

steps.heading: Create the acceptance criteria field
steps.body: Add a new field, typically a multi-line text or rich text type, named Acceptance Criteria.

steps.heading: Add it to the relevant screens
steps.body: Associate the field with the screens used for stories, so it appears when creating or editing that issue type.

steps.heading: Set a consistent format
steps.body: Encourage the team to use a consistent format, such as Given/When/Then, when filling in the field.

steps.heading: Reference it during review
steps.body: Use the field during backlog grooming and code review to confirm the story meets what was agreed upon.

faq.question: Should acceptance criteria be a separate field or part of the description?
faq.answer: A separate field keeps criteria consistent and easy to scan, rather than getting lost inside a longer description.

faq.question: What format works best for acceptance criteria?
faq.answer: Many teams use a Given/When/Then structure, though a simple bulleted checklist also works well.

faq.question: Can this field be required before a story is marked ready?
faq.answer: Yes, workflow validators can be configured to require the field is filled in before certain transitions are allowed.

faq.question: Who is responsible for writing acceptance criteria?
faq.answer: This is often the product owner's responsibility, sometimes refined together with the development team during grooming.
